pi_items.source: Task description states: 'Compose an email to audit-compliance@yourcompany.com with subject Inbox Domain Audit Report'
Task description states: 'create a dedicated IMAP folder named exactly Domain-<domainname> — for example, if the top domain is example.com, create a folder named Domain-example.com'
Task description explicitly requires: '(1) Analyze your inbox to identify the top 3 most frequently appearing sender domains' and '(2) For each of the top 3 domains, create a dedicated IMAP folder'

Verified by counting files in /examples/bluemail_env/assets/emails/ham/ - contains exactly 50 ham email files (ham_001.eml through ham_050.eml). Setup script loads all 50 emails into inbox: 'Load all 50 ham emails into inbox'

Analyzed all 50 ham email files by extracting From: headers and identifying sender domains. Domain frequency count: srv0.ems.ed.ac.uk (3 emails), eircom.net (3 emails), 2ubh.com (2 emails). This matches the domain extraction logic in setup_task.sh which uses Counter.most_common(3)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real-world-like email dataset containing 50 ham emails from /examples/bluemail_env/assets/emails/ham/. The top 3 sender domains by frequency are: srv0.ems.ed.ac.uk (3 occurrences), eircom.net (3 occurrences), and 2ubh.com (2 occurrences). The task metadata correctly specifies the audit recipient as audit-compliance@yourcompany.com, folder naming prefix as Domain-, and requirement to create folders for the top 3 domains (min_domain_folders: 3). All metadata claims match the task description and actual data.
